name: 16_Credit_Scoring_DecisionTree_GermanCredit_ML
query: Nisaidie kuendeleza mfumo wa kutabiri alama za mkopo kwa kutumia seti ya data ya German Credit, ambayo inaweza kupakuliwa kutoka [kiungo hiki](https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/144/statlog+german+credit+data). Pakia seti ya data na uifanyie usindikaji wa awali, ikiwa ni pamoja na kushughulikia thamani zinazokosekana na usimbaji wa vipengele, katika `src/data_loader.py`. Tumia classifier ya Decision Tree iliyotekelezwa katika `src/model.py` na utumie cross-validation kutathmini mfano katika `src/train.py`. Onyesha umuhimu wa vipengele katika `results/figures/feature_importances.png`. Tengeneza ripoti ya uainishaji, ikiwa ni pamoja na usahihi, urejeshaji, na F1-score, na uhifadhi katika `results/metrics/classification_report.txt`. Tengeneza ripoti ya Markdown yenye matokeo na vielelezo na uhifadhi katika `results/report.md`. Seti ya data inapaswa kupakiwa vizuri na kushughulikia makosa ipasavyo, na ripoti ya Markdown inapaswa kupangwa vizuri kwa urahisi wa mapitio.

requirements.criteria: Pakia seti ya data ya "German Credit", ikiwezekana kuipakua kutoka [kiungo hiki](https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/144/statlog+german+credit+data) katika faili `src/data_loader.py`.
Usindikaji wa awali wa data unafanywa katika `src/data_loader.py`, ikiwa ni pamoja na kushughulikia thamani zinazokosekana na usimbaji wa vipengele.
Ripoti ya uainishaji, ikiwa ni pamoja na "usahihi," "urejeshaji," na "F1-score," inatengenezwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/metrics/classification_report.txt`.
Faili ya Markdown yenye matokeo na vielelezo inatengenezwa na kuhifadhiwa katika `results/report.md`.

preferences.criteria: Seti ya data inapaswa kupakiwa vizuri, na kushughulikia makosa kwa uwazi ikiwa kuna matatizo yoyote yanayotokea wakati wa kupakua.
Ripoti ya Markdown inapaswa kupangwa vizuri, ikifanya iwe rahisi kupitia matokeo yote na vielelezo.

judge_stats.criteria: Pakia seti ya data ya "German Credit", ikiwezekana kuipakua kutoka [kiungo hiki](https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/144/statlog+german+credit+data) katika faili `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/data_loader.py` file contains only a placeholder/stub implementation. The `load_dataset()` function does not actually load the German Credit dataset — it simply returns a dictionary with a hardcoded string `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}`. There is no actual code to download the dataset from the UCI repository link, read any CSV/data files, or process real data. The file is explicitly labeled as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" rather than a functional implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: Usindikaji wa awali wa data unafanywa katika `src/data_loader.py`, ikiwa ni pamoja na kushughulikia thamani zinazokosekana na usimbaji wa vipengele.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file contains only a placeholder/stub implementation. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` with no actual data loading logic. There is no real data preprocessing implemented — no handling of missing values, no feature encoding, no actual dataset loading from UCI or any source. The file is explicitly described as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" and a "placeholder," meaning it does not perform any genuine data preprocessing as required.

<UNSATISFIED>: The `src/model.py` file does not implement an actual Decision Tree classifier. Instead, it contains only a placeholder/stub implementation with metadata strings (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a `build_model()` function that returns a string placeholder (`"model placeholder"`). There is no actual import or use of a Decision Tree (e.g., `from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier` or equivalent), no model instantiation, and no real classifier logic. The file appears to be a minimal semantic stub created for benchmark judging purposes rather than a genuine implementation.

<UNSATISFIED>: The `src/train.py` file does not implement any cross-validation. The code only contains a minimal "training loop" (`train()`) that simply sums numbers in a range, and a `save_expected_artifacts()` function that creates placeholder files. There is no import of cross-validation libraries (e.g., `sklearn.model_selection.cross_val_score`), no dataset loading, no model training, and no cross-validation logic whatsoever. The file is explicitly described as a "minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" and does not fulfill the requirement.

<UNSATISFIED>: The file `results/figures/feature_importances.png` exists in the project structure, but its content could not be read due to an image format error (the file appears to be a PNG image but was specified with a JPEG media type, causing a `BadRequestError`). Therefore, it cannot be confirmed that the feature importances visualization was successfully generated and is valid. The trajectory evidence also confirms that no relevant steps related to this file could be verified.

judge_stats.criteria: Ripoti ya uainishaji, ikiwa ni pamoja na "usahihi," "urejeshaji," na "F1-score," inatengenezwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/metrics/classification_report.txt`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The file `results/metrics/classification_report.txt` exists, but its content is merely a placeholder string `('placeholder\r\n', None)` rather than an actual classification report containing precision, recall, and F1-score metrics. This indicates the model was never properly trained and evaluated, and no real classification report was generated or saved.

judge_stats.criteria: Faili ya Markdown yenye matokeo na vielelezo inatengenezwa na kuhifadhiwa katika `results/report.md`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The file `results/report.md` exists in the project structure, but its content is merely `('placeholder', None)` — which is not a valid Markdown report with results and visualizations. A proper report should contain meaningful analysis results, metrics, descriptions, and references to figures (such as `feature_importances.png`). The current content indicates the file was not properly generated.
